perceptual_narrative_logs.visual_narrative_log: (1) A man with brown hair, wearing a bright orange shirt, stands on a red-brick balcony with white stone railings, holding a bright orange smartphone to his ear while smiling and speaking expressively. (2) He gestures with his free hand while leaning against the balcony railing, maintaining a boastful facial expression. (3) A hand wearing a blue and white striped shirt enters from the right and flips the phone over, revealing a photo gallery interface on the screen; the man's expression shifts to a neutral, slightly confused look as he stares at the camera.
perceptual_narrative_logs.audio_narrative_log: (1) Upbeat, medium-tempo electronic music starts along with a male voice speaking loudly and excitedly in Mandarin. (2) The male voice continues a continuous monologue with a boastful tone. (3) A sharp 'swish' or 'pop' sound effect occurs as the phone is flipped, followed by a brief pause in speech while the background music continues at the same intensity.
perceptual_narrative_logs.text_narrative_log: (1) Functional Text: 'iPhone 17 拿到手了' (Got my hands on the iPhone 17) - boastful and exciting tone. (2) Functional Text: '庫克上禮拜就給嘎哥了' (Tim Cook gave it to Ga Ge last week) - bragging tone. (3) Functional Text: '大家都在開箱了' (Everyone is doing unboxings) - informative tone.

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 1 main male creator, 1 hand of an off-screen person (likely female based on hand size/sleeve style)
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: A man with short brown hair, wearing an oversized bright orange t-shirt over a white shirt, dark blue denim-style shorts, white socks with red stripes, and red athletic shoes.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: A traditional red brick building with white stone balustrades and arched architectural details; greenery and trees are visible in the background.

narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video is a comedic short designed to parody technology unboxing culture and influencers who claim to have early access to products. The creator humorously brags about owning a non-existent 'iPhone 17' in an orange color, only to be immediately exposed when another person flips the phone to reveal it is just a digital image being held to his ear.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: The product shown is a fictional/prank version of an iPhone that does not exist in the market.

social_sociology_tags.uniqueSignatures: The creator uses a characteristic high-energy, comedic delivery typical of Taiwanese variety skits.
social_sociology_tags.freeDescribe: The contrast between the traditional architectural setting and the modern 'fake' tech gadget enhances the comedic effect.
